info.long_name: Measles virus detection rate in wastewater surveillance
info.short_description: Percentage of wastewater samples with measles virus detection
info.long_description: The detection rate represents the proportion of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across participating wastewater treatment facilities. This metric is calculated by dividing the total number of detections by the total number of samples collected and multiplying by 100. Higher detection rates may indicate increased measles virus circulation in the community served by the wastewater treatment facilities.
info.statement: In {location}, {value}% of wastewater samples tested positive for measles virus

vaccine_exemption_rate.long_name: Kindergarten non-medical MMR Vaccine Exemption Rate (County)
vaccine_exemption_rate.short_description: Percentage of kindergartners with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements at county level.
vaccine_exemption_rate.long_description: County-level percentage of kindergartners with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements. Compiled from state-reported exemption data spanning 2009–2024. County-level coverage varies by state, as not all states report at the county level. Data from Fattah et al. 2026 (JAMA).

info.short_description: Value for the measles-related measure identified by the source column. Units vary by source.
info.long_description: Combined county-level measles indicators from multiple sources in long format. The source column identifies the measure type: wastewater_detection_rate (% wastewater samples positive; CDC NWSS), vaccine_exemption_rate (% kindergartners with MMR exemptions; Kiang et al.), jhu_measles_cases (weekly confirmed case counts; JHU), mmr_coverage_healthmap (estimated % children with MMR coverage; HealthMap), wapo_county_vax_rate (county MMR vaccination rate; Washington Post). Units and time resolutions differ by source.

info.long_name: Measles virus detection rate in wastewater surveillance
info.short_description: Percentage of wastewater samples with measles virus detection
info.long_description: The detection rate represents the proportion of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across participating wastewater treatment facilities. This metric is calculated by dividing the total number of detections by the total number of samples collected and multiplying by 100. Higher detection rates may indicate increased measles virus circulation in the community served by the wastewater treatment facilities.
info.statement: In {location}, {value}% of wastewater samples tested positive for measles virus

vaccine_exemption_rate.short_description: Percentage of kindergartners with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements at state level.
vaccine_exemption_rate.long_description: State-level percentage of kindergartners with non-medical exemptions from MMR vaccination requirements. Compiled from state-reported exemption data spanning 2009–2024, covering prepandemic (2009–2019) and postpandemic (2020–2024) periods. Data from Fattah et al. 2026 (JAMA).

info.short_description: Value for the measles-related measure identified by the source column. Units vary by source.
info.long_description: Combined state-level measles indicators from multiple sources in long format. The source column identifies the measure type: wastewater_detection_rate (% wastewater samples positive for measles RNA; CDC NWSS), vaccine_exemption_rate (% kindergartners with MMR exemptions; Kiang et al.), jhu_measles_cases (weekly confirmed case counts from JHU), mmr_coverage_healthmap (estimated % children with MMR coverage; HealthMap), cdc_measles_cases (national weekly CDC case counts). Units and time resolutions differ by source.

info.long_name: Measles virus detection rate in wastewater surveillance
info.short_description: Percentage of wastewater samples with measles virus detection
info.long_description: The detection rate represents the proportion of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across participating wastewater treatment facilities. Higher detection rates may indicate increased measles virus circulation in the community served by the wastewater treatment facilities.

bundle_measles/dist/measles_state.parque.short_description: Value for the measles-related measure identified by the source column. Units vary by source.
bundle_measles/dist/measles_state.parque.long_description: Combined state-level measles indicators from multiple sources in long format. The source column identifies the measure type: wastewater_detection_rate (% wastewater samples positive for measles RNA; CDC NWSS), vaccine_exemption_rate (% kindergartners with MMR exemptions; Kiang et al.), jhu_measles_cases (weekly confirmed case counts from JHU), mmr_coverage_healthmap (estimated % children with MMR coverage; HealthMap), cdc_measles_cases (national weekly CDC case counts). The source column identifies the measure type: wastewater_detection_rate (% wastewater samples positive; CDC NWSS), vaccine_exemption_rate (% kindergartners with MMR exemptions; Kiang et al.), jhu_measles_cases (weekly confirmed case counts; JHU), mmr_coverage_healthmap (estimated % children with MMR coverage; HealthMap), wapo_county_vax_rate (county MMR vaccination rate; Washington Post).
